summarization.a: In-plane 2θχ XRD patterns of MnO₂ films lithiated from 100cLi to 300cLi, revealing the disappearance of β-MnO₂ reflections and a systematic shift of lithiated-phase peaks to lower angles with increasing lithium content.
summarization.b: Out-of-plane θ–2θ XRD patterns of the same samples, confirming full lithiation of thicker films and indicating lattice expansion and structural distortion in the lithiated phase.

|---|---|
| β-MnO₂ (100) | 20.3 |
| Li₁−xMn₂O₄ (111) | 17.7 |
| Li₁−xMn₂O₄ (311) | 35.9 |
| Li₁−xMn₂O₄ (400) | 44.4 |
| Li₂−zMnO₂ (012) * | 47.6 |
| β-MnO₂ (200) | 41.3 |

d.question: What does the evolution of the XRD patterns indicate about the phase composition and structural stability of MnO₂ films under high lithium concentrations?
d.answer: The XRD patterns show that at higher lithium concentrations (200cLi–MnO₂ and 300cLi–MnO₂), the β-MnO₂ phase disappears, indicating complete lithiation throughout the film thickness. The reflections of the lithiated phase shift systematically to lower angles, reflecting lattice expansion and possible one-dimensional distortion due to stress. Higher-order reflections further suggest the preservation of cubic symmetry with minor structural deformation. Together, these results indicate that lithium insertion significantly alters the phase composition while maintaining the overall crystal framework, though with some stress-induced distortion.

c.question: Which structural changes are observed in the XRD patterns upon increasing lithium content in the films?
c.answer: Disappearance of β-MnO₂ reflections, Shift of lithiated-phase peaks to lower 2θ angles, Appearance of higher-order reflections at multiples of the fundamental peak, Indication of lattice expansion and one-dimensional distortion due to stress

a.question: Does lithium fully penetrate the thicker MnO₂ films at 200cLi–MnO₂ and 300cLi–MnO₂ concentrations?

b.question: How do the 2θ positions of the lithiated phase reflections shift as lithium concentration increases?
